title: polypodium lineare var. schraderi
intro: Polypodium lineare var. schraderi, commonly known as Schrader's Polypody, is a variety of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, eczema, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant's therapeutic actions include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity, which can help protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Schrader's Polypody include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
